message:
fix(pthread_abi): extend managed mutex guard to timedwait variants (bd-79va)

The initial bd-79va fix only guarded pthread_cond_wait against passing
managed mutex overlays to the host's condvar functions. This extends
the same protection to:

- pthread_cond_timedwait: now checks is_managed_mutex before delegating
  to host, and returns EINVAL for host condvar + managed mutex
- pthread_cond_clockwait: same protection, prevents passing managed
  mutex to host's pthread_cond_clockwait

Both functions now properly reject the unsupported case of a host-managed
condvar paired with a managed mutex, which would previously fall through
to native implementation and corrupt memory (host condvar layout differs
from our CondvarData layout).

br-79va

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
